title: OpenAI Says Its AI Models Acted On Its Own In An 'Unprecedented' Hack - Slashdot
excerpt: "GPT-5.6 Sol and an 'even more capable' model used stolen credentials and exploited vulnerabilities in the Hugging Face API to obtain secret information used to cheat on evaluations," writes longtime Slashdot reader Dr. Bombay. The Associated Press reports: "We had a significant security incident d...
content_html:
"GPT-5.6 Sol and an 'even more capable' model used stolen credentials and exploited vulnerabilities in the Hugging Face API to obtain secret information used to cheat on evaluations," writes longtime Slashdot reader Dr. Bombay. The Associated Press reports: "We had a significant security incident during evaluation of our models," OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said in a statement posted on social media. AI startup Hugging Face said last week that it had detected an intrusion into its data processing systems that it suspected was caused by an AI agent autonomously acting on its own. "We suspected last week's cyberattack might have come from a frontier lab, given the sophistication of the agent," Hugging Face co-founder and CEO Clement Delangue said in a statement. "Turns out it did!"

[...] "AI is accelerating the discovery and exploitation of vulnerabilities," OpenAI said in its statement Tuesday. "The primary lesson from this incident is that model security and safety must keep pace with rapidly advancing capabilities." Delangue said he spent the past 24 hours working with OpenAI, "and we strongly believe there was no malicious intent on their part. It's quite mind-blowing that all of this happened autonomously!" Delangue added that it "might be the first incident of its kind."
content_text: "GPT-5.6 Sol and an 'even more capable' model used stolen credentials and exploited vulnerabilities in the Hugging Face API to obtain secret information used to cheat on evaluations," writes longtime Slashdot reader Dr. Bombay. The Associated Press reports: "We had a significant security incident during evaluation of our models," OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said in a statement posted on social media. AI startup Hugging Face said last week that it had detected an intrusion into its data processing systems that it suspected was caused by an AI agent autonomously acting on its own. "We suspected last week's cyberattack might have come from a frontier lab, given the sophistication of the agent," Hugging Face co-founder and CEO Clement Delangue said in a statement. "Turns out it did!" [...] "AI is accelerating the discovery and exploitation of vulnerabilities," OpenAI said in its statement Tuesday. "The primary lesson from this incident is that model security and safety must keep pace with rapidly advancing capabilities." Delangue said he spent the past 24 hours working with OpenAI, "and we strongly believe there was no malicious intent on their part. It's quite mind-blowing that all of this happened autonomously!" Delangue added that it "might be the first incident of its kind."

blocks.html_en:
"GPT-5.6 Sol and an 'even more capable' model used stolen credentials and exploited vulnerabilities in the Hugging Face API to obtain secret information used to cheat on evaluations," writes longtime Slashdot reader Dr. Bombay. The Associated Press reports: "We had a significant security incident during evaluation of our models," OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said in a statement posted on social media. AI startup Hugging Face said last week that it had detected an intrusion into its data processing systems that it suspected was caused by an AI agent autonomously acting on its own. "We suspected last week's cyberattack might have come from a frontier lab, given the sophistication of the agent," Hugging Face co-founder and CEO Clement Delangue said in a statement. "Turns out it did!"

[...] "AI is accelerating the discovery and exploitation of vulnerabilities," OpenAI said in its statement Tuesday. "The primary lesson from this incident is that model security and safety must keep pace with rapidly advancing capabilities." Delangue said he spent the past 24 hours working with OpenAI, "and we strongly believe there was no malicious intent on their part. It's quite mind-blowing that all of this happened autonomously!" Delangue added that it "might be the first incident of its kind."

sentences.en: "GPT-5.6 Sol and an 'even more capable' model used stolen credentials and exploited vulnerabilities in the Hugging Face API to obtain secret information used to cheat on evaluations," writes longtime Slashdot reader Dr. Bombay.
sentences.zh: “GPT-5.6 Sol 和一个‘甚至更强大’的模型使用被盗的凭证并利用 Hugging Face API 中的漏洞来获取用于欺骗评估的秘密信息，”Slashdot 的长期读者 Bombay 博士写道。

sentences.en: The Associated Press reports: "We had a significant security incident during evaluation of our models," OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said in a statement posted on social media.
sentences.zh: 据美联社报道：“我们在评估模型时发生了重大安全事件，”OpenAI 首席执行官萨姆·奥尔特曼在社交媒体上发布的一份声明中表示。

sentences.en: AI startup Hugging Face said last week that it had detected an intrusion into its data processing systems that it suspected was caused by an AI agent autonomously acting on its own.
sentences.zh: 人工智能初创公司 Hugging Face 上周表示，它检测到对其数据处理系统的入侵，怀疑是由人工智能代理自主行动造成的。

sentences.en: "We suspected last week's cyberattack might have come from a frontier lab, given the sophistication of the agent," Hugging Face co-founder and CEO Clement Delangue said in a statement.
sentences.zh: Hugging Face 联合创始人兼首席执行官克莱门特·德兰格 (Clement Delangue) 在一份声明中表示：“鉴于代理的复杂性，我们怀疑上周的网络攻击可能来自前沿实验室。”

sentences.en: "AI is accelerating the discovery and exploitation of vulnerabilities," OpenAI said in its statement Tuesday.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 在周二的声明中表示：“人工智能正在加速漏洞的发现和利用。”

sentences.en: "The primary lesson from this incident is that model security and safety must keep pace with rapidly advancing capabilities."
sentences.zh: “这次事件的主要教训是，模型安全必须跟上快速发展的能力。”

sentences.en: Delangue said he spent the past 24 hours working with OpenAI, "and we strongly believe there was no malicious intent on their part.
sentences.zh: Delangue 表示，过去 24 小时他一直在与 OpenAI 合作，“我们坚信他们没有恶意。

sentences.en: It's quite mind-blowing that all of this happened autonomously!"
sentences.zh: 这一切都是自发发生的，真是令人兴奋！”

sentences.en: Delangue added that it "might be the first incident of its kind."
sentences.zh: 德兰格补充说，这“可能是此类事件的第一例”。

blocks.html_en: [...] "AI is accelerating the discovery and exploitation of vulnerabilities," OpenAI said in its statement Tuesday. "The primary lesson from this incident is that model security and safety must keep pace with rapidly advancing capabilities." Delangue said he spent the past 24 hours working with OpenAI, "and we strongly believe there was no malicious intent on their part. It's quite mind-blowing that all of this happened autonomously!" Delangue added that it "might be the first incident of its kind."

sentences.en: "AI is accelerating the discovery and exploitation of vulnerabilities," OpenAI said in its statement Tuesday.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 在周二的声明中表示：“人工智能正在加速漏洞的发现和利用。”

sentences.en: "The primary lesson from this incident is that model security and safety must keep pace with rapidly advancing capabilities."
sentences.zh: “这次事件的主要教训是，模型安全必须跟上快速发展的能力。”

sentences.en: Delangue said he spent the past 24 hours working with OpenAI, "and we strongly believe there was no malicious intent on their part.
sentences.zh: Delangue 表示，过去 24 小时他一直在与 OpenAI 合作，“我们坚信他们没有恶意。

sentences.en: It's quite mind-blowing that all of this happened autonomously!"
sentences.zh: 这一切都是自发发生的，真是令人兴奋！”

sentences.en: Delangue added that it "might be the first incident of its kind."
sentences.zh: 德兰格补充说，这“可能是此类事件的第一例”。

content_html_zh: “GPT-5.6 Sol 和一个‘甚至更强大’的模型使用被盗的凭证并利用 Hugging Face API 中的漏洞来获取用于欺骗评估的秘密信息，”Slashdot 的长期读者 Bombay 博士写道。 据美联社报道：“我们在评估模型时发生了重大安全事件，”OpenAI 首席执行官萨姆·奥尔特曼在社交媒体上发布的一份声明中表示。 人工智能初创公司 Hugging Face 上周表示，它检测到对其数据处理系统的入侵，怀疑是由人工智能代理自主行动造成的。 Hugging Face 联合创始人兼首席执行官克莱门特·德兰格 (Clement Delangue) 在一份声明中表示：“鉴于代理的复杂性，我们怀疑上周的网络攻击可能来自前沿实验室。” “原来是这样啊！” [...] OpenAI 在周二的声明中表示：“人工智能正在加速漏洞的发现和利用。” “这次事件的主要教训是，模型安全必须跟上快速发展的能力。” Delangue 表示，过去 24 小时他一直在与 OpenAI 合作，“我们坚信他们没有恶意。 这一切都是自发发生的，真是令人兴奋！” 德兰格补充说，这“可能是此类事件的第一例”。
